title: Intro to Transformers
text: Transformers are deep learning models primarily used for NLP. They process sequences in parallel using self-attention, unlike RNNs which process sequentially. Transformers have become the foundation of large language models.

title: Self-Attention Mechanism
text: Self-attention allows a model to weigh the importance of each token in a sequence relative to others. This mechanism helps transformers understand context and relationships within text.

title: RAG Systems
text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems combine a retriever model with a generator. The retriever fetches relevant documents, which the generator uses to produce informed responses. RAG improves accuracy in LLM outputs.

title: LangChain Overview
text: LangChain is a framework for building LLM applications. It supports prompt templates, chaining LLMs, and integrating with document retrieval systems. LangChain simplifies complex LLM workflows.

title: OpenAI Embeddings
text: 'text-embedding-3-large' is an OpenAI embedding model that produces 3072-dimensional vectors. These embeddings capture semantic information from text and are used for tasks like semantic search or clustering.

title: MCP Protocol Overview
text: MCP is a network communication protocol designed for secure and reliable message passing between distributed systems. It emphasizes low-latency delivery and fault tolerance.

title: Prompt Templates
text: Prompt templates in LangChain allow you to define reusable patterns for queries to LLMs. Templates help standardize prompts and dynamically insert variables for different tasks.

title: LLM Chains
text: LLM chains are sequences of steps combining multiple LLM calls or retrieval processes. Chains can include logic like conditional branching, data transformation, and multi-step reasoning.

title: Embedding Usage in Retrieval
text: Embeddings map text to high-dimensional vectors. In RAG pipelines, the retriever searches the embedding space to find relevant documents that match the semantic meaning of a query.

title: Chunking for RAG
text: Chunking splits documents into smaller overlapping pieces. Overlaps ensure context is preserved, which improves retrieval quality and relevance in RAG pipelines.
